text:
PET-CT WHOLE BODY WITH INTEGRATED LOW-DOSE CT
PRE-TRANSPLANT RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Ms. Margaret Chen is a 48-year-old female with recently diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FLT3-ITD positive with high allelic ratio, adverse ELN 2022 risk. She completed 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin) with midostaurin and achieved morphologic complete remission confirmed by day +28 bone marrow biopsy. This PET-CT is obtained for pre-allogeneic stem cell transplant restaging to evaluate for any residual FDG-avid disease and to assess interval change from staging CT performed approximately 6 weeks prior. A 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor has been identified; transplant planning is underway.

TECHNIQUE:
Whole-body PET-CT examination was performed from vertex to mid-thigh following intravenous administration of approximately 10.2 mCi (377 MBq) of fluorine-18 fluorodeoxyglucose (FDG). The patient fasted for greater than 6 hours prior to tracer injection. Serum glucose at time of injection: 91 mg/dL. Uptake phase: 61 minutes. Images were acquired in 3D mode from vertex to mid-thigh. Integrated low-dose CT was performed for attenuation correction and anatomic correlation. CT scan performed without intravenous contrast given adequate IV access was not available at the time of scanning; scout views and low-dose CT reviewed in conjunction with dedicated diagnostic CT with contrast performed at outside facility approximately 6 weeks prior. PET images reconstructed using OSEM algorithm with resolution recovery. Reviewed on dedicated PET-CT workstation in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with maximum intensity projection (MIP) images.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast, obtained at initial AML staging (approximately 6 weeks prior to current study). No prior PET scan available for direct comparison.

FINDINGS:

GENERAL BACKGROUND ACTIVITY:
Physiologic FDG uptake is seen in the brain, myocardium, liver, spleen, gastrointestinal tract, kidneys, and urinary bladder, all within expected limits for the patient's age and clinical status. Background hepatic SUVmax is 2.3 (reference). Mediastinal blood pool SUVmax measured at the aortic arch is 1.6. No diffuse skeletal marrow hypermetabolism to suggest extensive active marrow repopulation. The pattern of marrow uptake is consistent with recovering or predominantly fatty marrow in the post-chemotherapy setting, which is expected and appropriate given the timing following induction.

HEAD AND NECK:
No FDG-avid cervical, submandibular, or parotid lymphadenopathy. No abnormal soft tissue metabolic activity in the oropharynx, hypopharynx, or neck. The salivary glands and thyroid demonstrate physiologic or mildly suppressed uptake consistent with post-chemotherapy state. No leptomeningeal or parenchymal FDG uptake is identified on the limited brain images included in the acquisition field. Brain parenchyma shows symmetric, physiologically intense FDG uptake throughout the cortex, basal ganglia, and cerebellum without focal areas of abnormal hypermetabolism or photopenia. No discrete skull base lesion.

CHEST:
Lungs: No FDG-avid pulmonary nodules or consolidations are identified. The lungs appear clear on the integrated CT images without new parenchymal opacities. The previously noted bilateral lower lobe consolidations documented during the febrile neutropenia episode are not identifiable as discrete lesions on today's study; there is no residual FDG activity in either lower lobe to suggest active infectious or inflammatory process. No pleural effusions are seen bilaterally; the small bilateral pleural effusions noted on the prior staging CT have resolved. No pleural thickening or pleural-based FDG activity.

Mediastinum and Hila: No enlarged or FDG-avid mediastinal, paratracheal, aortopulmonary window, subcarinal, or hilar lymph nodes are identified. The mediastinal vascular structures appear normal in caliber. No anterior mediastinal mass or thymic enlargement with abnormal hypermetabolism. No pericardial effusion or pericardial FDG activity.

Heart: Normal-appearing myocardial FDG uptake pattern. No regional wall motion abnormality suggested by metabolic distribution. No pericardial abnormality.

Breast Tissue: No focal FDG-avid breast lesion bilaterally.

Axillae: No abnormally enlarged or metabolically active axillary lymph nodes bilaterally.

ABDOMEN:
Liver: The liver is mildly enlarged, measuring approximately 15.3 cm in the craniocaudal dimension in the midclavicular line — this represents a notable decrease compared with the 17.1 cm measured at initial staging, indicating interval regression consistent with treatment response. The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous FDG uptake at a level appropriate for background reference activity. No focal hepatic lesion with abnormal hypermetabolism or with photopenic defect suggesting infiltrative disease or space-occupying lesion. The intrahepatic biliary ducts are not dilated. No ascites.

Spleen: The spleen has decreased substantially in size compared with the prior staging imaging. It now measures approximately 11.8 cm in the greatest craniocaudal dimension, down from 14.2 cm on the staging CT performed approximately 6 weeks earlier — a reduction of roughly 2.4 cm and now approaching normal size. The splenic parenchyma demonstrates mildly increased but relatively uniform FDG activity, consistent with expected post-chemotherapy reactive change and recovering hematopoiesis rather than active leukemic infiltration. There are no focal splenic lesions with distinctly abnormal FDG uptake to suggest discrete leukemic deposits or infarction.

Pancreas: Normal FDG uptake throughout the pancreatic head, body, and tail. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

Kidneys and Adrenal Glands: Physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems bilaterally. No focal renal mass. Adrenal glands are normal in size and metabolic activity without evidence of adrenal involvement.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Physiologic and variable FDG activity throughout the stomach, small bowel, and large bowel within expected limits. No focal region of abnormally intense bowel wall activity to suggest leukemic infiltration, active colitis, or occult neoplasm. The appendix is not separately visualized but no right lower quadrant abnormality is present.

Retroperitoneum and Abdominal Lymph Nodes: No FDG-avid para-aortic, aortocaval, paracaval, mesenteric, or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. The aorta and inferior vena cava appear normal in caliber without adjacent FDG-avid soft tissue.

PELVIS:
Pelvic Organs: The uterus and adnexa demonstrate expected physiologic FDG uptake without focal hypermetabolic lesion. The urinary bladder contains physiologic tracer activity. No pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No FDG-avid iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. No pelvic soft tissue mass with metabolic activity.

MUSCULOSKELETAL AND BONE MARROW:
The axial and appendicular skeleton demonstrates diffusely decreased, patchy marrow FDG activity compared with what would be expected in a normal physiologic state, consistent with post-chemotherapy bone marrow suppression in the recovery phase. This pattern is appropriate and expected in the context of recent myeloablative induction chemotherapy. There is no focal area of intensely increased marrow FDG uptake in the spine, pelvis, sternum, ribs, proximal femora, humeri, or calvarium to suggest focal leukemic infiltration, pathologic fracture, or new lytic osseous lesion. No blastic or lytic lesions are identified on the integrated CT images. No evidence of extramedullary hematopoiesis-related chloromatous (granulocytic sarcoma) deposits within the bone or adjacent soft tissue.

SOFT TISSUES:
No FDG-avid soft tissue masses, subcutaneous deposits, or skin lesions. No evidence of muscle or fascia involvement. The imaged portions of the chest wall, abdominal wall, and pelvic soft tissues are unremarkable.

IMPRESSION:

1. Complete metabolic response to induction chemotherapy. There is no FDG-avid focus anywhere in the body to suggest viable residual leukemic disease, extramedullary disease, or granulocytic sarcoma. This finding is consistent with the morphologic and flow cytometry-confirmed complete remission documented on the concurrent day +28 bone marrow biopsy.

2. Marked interval improvement in splenomegaly. The spleen has decreased from 14.2 cm at initial staging to 11.8 cm on the current study, now approaching the upper limit of normal range, consistent with resolution of prior leukemic infiltration of the spleen.

3. Interval normalization of hepatomegaly. Liver size has decreased from 17.1 cm at initial staging to 15.3 cm, consistent with resolution of prior hepatic involvement.

4. Complete resolution of the previously noted bilateral lower lobe pulmonary consolidations (febrile neutropenia-related pneumonitis/pneumonia during induction). No residual FDG activity in either lower lobe. The previously identified small bilateral pleural effusions have also resolved.

5. Post-chemotherapy bone marrow pattern with diffusely diminished marrow FDG uptake consistent with marrow recovery following induction. No focal lesion or extramedullary disease identified.

6. No lymphadenopathy in the neck, mediastinum, axillae, abdomen, or pelvis.

Overall, these findings are reassuring for sustained complete metabolic remission and support proceeding with planned allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

Electronically signed by: Attending Radiologist, Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging
Resident radiologist participating in interpretation.
